uplink.description: Measurement uplink with ambient temperature + humidity (climate) plus enclosure diagnostics
uplink._note: ORACLE: synthetic in-bounds TLV (channel 0xDD): 0xFE deviceTimestamp, 0x81 ambientTemperature raw 2345 x0.01 = 23.45 C, 0x82 ambientHumidity 47%, 0x71 internalCircuitTemperature raw 2100 x0.01 = 21 C, 0x72 internalCircuitHumidity 41%. expected.data authored from running reference/upstream-codec.js on this input, then normalized (ambient -> air.*; upstream version-control fields dropped).

uplink.description: Basic information uplink (fPort 50): battery voltage + percentage, device class, power event
uplink._note: ORACLE: synthetic basic-info TLV (channel 0xFF): 0x07 batteryPercentage 88, 0x08 batteryVoltage raw 36 x0.1 = 3.6 V, 0x11 deviceClass 0x02 = Class C, 0x06 powerEvent 0x01 = AC Power On. expected.data from reference, then normalized: battery voltage -> vocabulary battery (V), percentage -> batteryPercent extra.

uplink.description: Electricity + voltage/current + P1 slave-channel reading (all metering extras, no vocab key)
uplink._note: ORACLE: synthetic measurement TLV: 0x06 electricityDeliveredToClientT1 1000 Wh, 0x22 voltageL1 raw 2300 x0.1 = 230 V, 0x26 currentL1 5 A, 0x50 lastReadingOnChannel1 (P1 slave, e.g. gas) ts 1730794800 value 12345. expected.data from reference, normalized to camelCase extras: the vocabulary has no electricity/gas-metering key and metering.water.total is WATER volume only, so these are preserved verbatim as extras.
ORACLE: fPort 99 is outside the info (50) and measurement (1-10) ranges. Upstream returns {data:{error:'Incorrect fPort'}}; this codec returns {errors:[...]} per the output contract.
ORACLE: defensive guard for a zero-length byte array (not exercised by upstream, which would index undefined).
